evals.prompt: I have a PDF report at ~/Downloads/report.pdf, convert it to markdown and save as report.md in the same folder.
evals.expected_output: Uses markitdown CLI or Python API to convert the PDF to markdown. Checks if markitdown is installed first, runs the conversion, and saves the output to ~/Downloads/report.md.

assertions.name: uses-markitdown
assertions.description: Response uses markitdown (not an alternative like pdftotext, pypdf2, or pandoc) as the primary conversion tool

assertions.name: checks-installation
assertions.description: Response checks or mentions verifying that markitdown is installed before running the conversion

assertions.name: correct-output-path
assertions.description: Output file path is ~/Downloads/report.md (same directory as input)

assertions.name: provides-runnable-command
assertions.description: Contains a concrete CLI command or Python snippet that could actually be executed

evals.prompt: Convert all .xlsx files in my current directory to markdown tables.
evals.expected_output: Writes a batch conversion script (bash loop or Python with pathlib) that converts every .xlsx file to a .md file using markitdown. Handles multiple files efficiently rather than one-off CLI calls.

assertions.name: uses-markitdown
assertions.description: Uses markitdown for the conversion, not openpyxl/pandas/xlrd alone

assertions.name: handles-multiple-files
assertions.description: Uses a loop, glob pattern, or wildcard rather than a single hardcoded filename

assertions.name: output-files-named-correctly
assertions.description: Each .xlsx input maps to a corresponding .md output filename (e.g. data.xlsx -> data.md)

assertions.name: provides-runnable-code
assertions.description: Code or script is complete and could be run without modification

evals.prompt: Turn this page into markdown: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Markdown
evals.expected_output: Uses markitdown to fetch and convert the URL to markdown. Either runs `markitdown <url>` in the CLI or uses the Python API's convert() method on the URL string.

assertions.name: uses-markitdown
assertions.description: Uses markitdown to convert the URL, not wget+pandoc, requests+BeautifulSoup, or other scraping approaches

assertions.name: url-passed-directly
assertions.description: The Wikipedia URL is passed directly to markitdown rather than first downloading the HTML separately

assertions.name: output-is-markdown
assertions.description: The response either prints, saves, or shows a sample of the resulting markdown content
